data.title: Sanofi multiple sclerosis therapy rejected by the FDA
data.description:
Sanofi (SNY) announced on Wednesday that the U.S. FDA issued a complete response letter regarding its marketing application for tolebrutinib, rejecting the drug for patients with multiple sclerosis, an autoimmune condition affecting the central nervous system.

The decision came after the French drugmaker said last week that it will not seek regulatory approval for tolebrutinib in primary progressive multiple sclerosis following the drug’s failure in a late-stage trial.

SNY’s rejected new drug application was related to tolebrutinib's use in adults with non-relapsing secondary progressive multiple sclerosis (nrSPMS).

According to Sanofi (SNY), the FDA has recently stated that its review for the Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor could extend beyond the Dec. 18 target action date, and it would provide further guidance in Q1 2026.

The rejection “is a significant and meaningful change in direction from the feedback the agency previously provided to Sanofi,” the company said in a statement. “We remain committed to working with the FDA to find a path forward for tolebrutinib and ultimately serve the MS community.”

SNY added that with its Q4 results in January, it will issue an update on an impairment test evaluating the intangible asset value attached to tolebrutinib. The outcome of the ongoing test will have an impact on the company’s outlook for 2025.

The announcement came minutes before Sanofi (SNY) disclosed an agreement to acquire the U.S. vaccine maker Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) in an all-cash deal worth nearly $2.2B.

data.title: Stock futures little changed ahead of truncated Christmas Eve trading session
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were mostly flat on Wednesday, ahead of a shortened regular trading session due to Christmas Eve.

Markets will close at 1300 ET today and will be shut on Thursday.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced +0.06%. Dow futures (INDU) rose +0.09%, and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) edged up +0.01%.

A blowout U.S. Q3 GDP report lifted sentiment in the previous session and helped Wall Street notch its 38th record close of the year, underscoring the stock market's resilience after a turbulent 2025. .

See the chart below shared by Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, on X:

Wednesday also marks the start of a period that has traditionally resulted in gains for equities, leading to a Santa Claus rally - a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year.  

Treasury yields slipped in premarket trading. The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell to 4.16%. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) eased to 3.53%. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) declined to 4.82%.

Top S&P 500 gainers premarket included Nike (NKE) +2.23%, Textron (TXT) +1.66%, and TKO (TKO) +1.41%. Decliners included Intel (INTC) -3.60%, Bio-Techne (TECH) -1.92%, and FirstEnergy (FE) -1.84%.

data.title: Nike gains after Apple's Tim Cook buys the dip
data.description:
Nike (NKE) rallied in early trading on Wednesday after disclosing a new round of insider buying of the company's stock.

Independent director Tim Cook bought 50,000 shares at $58.97, worth approximately $2.95 million, on December 22. Cook currently serves as lead independent director and chair of the board's compensation committee. He is a longtime executive of Apple (AAPL) and the current CEO of the tech giant.

Independent director Robert Swan bought 8,691 shares at $57.54, worth approximately $500K, on December 22. Swan serves as chair of Nike's Audit & Finance Committee and is designated an "audit committee financial expert" under SEC rules.

Analysts said the insider buying action signals confidence in Nike's (NKE) turnaround and "Win Now" strategy under CEO Elliott Hill.

Shares of Nike (NKE) were up 2.2% in premarket trading to $58.62 vs. the 52-week range of $52.28 to $82.44. On a year-to-date basis, Nike (NKE) is down 24.2%.

data.title: Global airline traffic is forecast to rise 4.9% in 2026
data.description:
The International Air Transport Association forecasts a 4.9% year-over-year growth in passenger traffic, led by the Asia Pacific region’s expansion by 7.3%. The traffic growth is a marginal deceleration over the pace seen for the first ten months of 2025 due mainly to persistent supply-side constraints, including limited aircraft availability and labor shortages. 

IATA also noted that supply constraints continue to keep load factors at record highs, projected at 83.8%, which in turn supports yields and profits in an otherwise turbulent operating environment. Resilient traffic growth and stable yields are expected to allow the global airline industry to top the $1 trillion level in 2025, which would mark an industry first.

Looking at profitability, record high load factors and fleet utilization, together with a rapid expansion of ancillary revenues, are anticipated to allow airlines to maintain a relatively healthy profit amid the headwinds. IATA's forecast calls for a record high net profit of $41 billion for 2026 and a stable net margin of 3.9%. "This impressive achievement should be possible despite softening fares and continuous cost pressure. It must be said, though, that the airline industry remains a low-margin industry. At the higher end of the spectrum, single companies can realize our entire industry’s total annual profit in a single quarter," highlighted IATA.

Global airline stocks: Delta Air Lines (DAL), American Airlines (AAL), Lufthansa (DLAKY) (DLAKF), United Airlines (UAL), International Consolidated Airlines Group (ICAGY), Air France-KLM Group (AFLYY) (AFRAF), Southwest Airlines (LUV), Spirit Airlines (SAVEQ), Alaska Airlines (ALK), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), Sun Country (SNCY), Frontier Air Group (ULCC), Hawaiian Airlines (HA), Skywest (SKYW), Allegiant (ALGT), Mesa Air Group (MESA), Air Canada (ACDVF), Wizz Air (WZZAF), easyJet (EJTTF), Ryanair (RYAAY), Republic Airways Holdings (RJET), SAS Group (SASDQ), Jet2 (DRTGF), KLM Royal Dutch Airlines (KLMR), Aeroflot (AERZY), Aegean Airlines S.A. (AGZNF), Finnair (FNNNF), Norwegian Air (NWARF), Air China (AIRYY) (AICAF), Volaris (VLRS), Copa Holdings (CPA), LATAM Airlines (LTM), TUI AG (TUIFF), China Eastern Airline (CHEAF), China Souhern Airlines (CHKIF), Cathay Pacific (CPCAY) (CPCAF), ANA Holdings (ALNPY), Japan Airlines (JPNRF) (JPNRF), El AL Israel Airlines (ELALF), Singapore Airlines (SINGY), Thai Airways (TAWNF),  and Qantas Airways (QABSY).

data.title: Italy's antitrust body orders Meta to allow rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp
data.description:
Italy's competition authority Autorità Garante della Concorrenza e del Mercato or AGCM, ordered Meta (META) to suspend WhatsApp's contractual terms that block rival AI chatbots from the platform.

The step addresses concerns over potential abuse of Meta's (META) dominant position in the AI chatbot market, and the order came amid an ongoing investigation launched in July 2025 and expanded in November.

A spokesperson for Meta (META) called the decision “fundamentally flawed,” and said the emergence of AI chatbots “put a strain on our systems that they were not designed to ​support,” Reuters reported. “We will appeal,” the spokesperson added.

"Meta’s conduct appears to constitute an abuse, since it may limit production, market access or technical developments in the AI Chatbot services market, to the detriment of consumers," the authority said.

The Italian regulators are also coordinating with the European Commission, which launched a parallel EU probe last month.

data.title: Blackrock Silver to raise C$15M in non-brokered offering at C$1.10 per unit
Biggest stock movers Wednesday: DVAX, NKE, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged modestly higher Wednesday morning, building on the S&P 500's fresh record close from Tuesday, ahead of an early market shutdown at 1:00 p.m. ET for Christmas Eve.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) +37% - Shares surged after Sanofi (SNY) agreed to acquire the vaccine maker for about $2.2B, or $15.50 per share in cash, representing a 39% premium to its December 23 close. This deal strengthens Sanofi’s adult immunization portfolio with Dynavax’s marketed HEPLISAV-B hepatitis B vaccine and its phase 1/2 shingles candidate Z-1018. Sanofi will fund the transaction entirely with cash and launch a tender offer followed by a merger if successful. It expects no impact on its 2025 guidance and anticipates closing in Q1 2026, with Dynavax CEO Ryan Spencer saying the combination will accelerate the global reach and impact of the company’s vaccine portfolio.
  • UiPath (PATH) +6% - Shares climbed after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the automation software company will join the S&P MidCap 400, replacing Synovus Financial ((SNV)), effective January 2, 2026, following Synovus’s pending acquisition by Pinnacle Financial Partners ((PNFP)), a move that is expected to drive incremental demand from index-tracking funds.
  • Nike (NKE) +2% - Shares rose and drew investor attention after a regulatory filing showed notable insider buying, led by Apple CEO and Nike’s lead independent director Tim Cook, who purchased nearly $3M worth of stock. According to an SEC Form 4, Cook bought 50,000 Class B shares on December 22 at an average price of $58.97, taking his direct holdings to 105,480 shares and signaling confidence in Nike’s long-term outlook. Adding to the bullish insider signal, another Nike director and former Intel CEO Robert Swan also bought 8,691 shares the same day at an average price of $57.54, reinforcing optimism around the embattled sportswear company.
